headline: Industrial Stocks Are Getting Hammered. Here’s Why.
summary: BRP is short for Bombardier Recreational Products.  Tuesday, the company suspended fiscal year 2027 guidance due to the recent changes to Section 232 steel, aluminum, and copper tariffs, which went into effect on April 6.  President Donald Trump used Section 232 to put 50% import tariffs on raw materials such as steel.

content.title: Industrial Stocks Are Getting Hammered. Here’s Why.
content.summary: BRP is short for Bombardier Recreational Products.  Tuesday, the company suspended fiscal year 2027 guidance due to the recent changes to Section 232 steel, aluminum, and copper tariffs, which went into effect on April 6.  President Donald Trump used Section 232 to put 50% import tariffs on raw materials such as steel.

headline: ASX Growth Companies With High Insider Ownership For April 2026
summary: As Australian shares continue to climb amid global uncertainties, including ongoing tensions in the Middle East and concerns about fuel shortages, investors are keenly observing how these factors might impact market dynamics. In such a volatile environment, growth companies with high insider ownership often attract attention due to the confidence insiders demonstrate in their business prospects, offering potential resilience and alignment of interests with shareholders.

headline: Tech Stocks Power S&P 500 and Nasdaq to Records
summary: Surging tech stocks carried the S&P 500 and Nasdaq composite to new records on Wednesday, a milestone in major indexes’ rebound from war-fueled losses.  Buoyed by big gains from the likes of  Microsoft  and  Tesla  the S&P 500 rose 0.8% to 7022.95—rallying into the close to finish comfortably above its previous all-time high set in January.  More recently, though, investors have grown increasingly confident that the U.S. and Iran will eventually reach a peace deal.

More recently, though, investors have grown increasingly confident that the U.S. and Iran will eventually reach a peace deal.
